title: Does Reading During the Summer Build Reading Skills? Evidence from a Randomized Experiment in 463 Classrooms
simulatability_note: Field experiment that measures the real-world reading comprehension test scores of students over the course of a summer break.

title: Paying for Kidneys? A Randomized Survey and Choice Experiment
simulatability_note: Online survey presenting text-based vignettes regarding alternative kidney procurement systems and measuring policy support and moral views.

title: The Choice Architecture of School Choice Websites
simulatability_note: Study fundamentally tests visual graphical user interfaces, maps, icons, and layout combinations, which cannot be accurately simulated by a text-only LLM.

title: Deliberative Polls
simulatability_note: Experiment relies heavily on in-person, multi-day, real-time group deliberation and interpersonal discussion among a live sample.

title: Charitable giving and Freedom from Hunger
simulatability_note: Direct-mail field experiment that measures actual monetary charitable donations sent via physical mail over several months.

title: Labor Market Concerns and Support for Immigration
simulatability_note: Online survey experiment measuring changes in policy preferences after reading text-based research evidence.

title: Does Promoting One Behavior Detract From Others? Evidence from a Field Experiment
simulatability_note: Real-world field experiment tracking participant engagement in physical behaviors (meditation and meal logging) over 4 weeks via smartphone apps.

title: Nudging Study Habits: A Field Experiment in Higher Education
simulatability_note: Field experiment tracking real students' uptake of university tutoring and academic support services.

title: Willingness to pay for a positive externality
simulatability_note: Online survey eliciting willingness-to-pay via a text-based BDM mechanism.

title: Predicting insurance demand from risk attitudes
simulatability_note: Online survey utilizing hypothetical lotteries and insurance demand questions that can be presented as text prompts.

title: The relative income effect: an experiment
simulatability_note: Online experiment randomly assigning point rewards and assessing self-reported subjective well-being (mood) via text surveys.

title: Exercise Habits Using Incentives: Flexibility and Routinization
simulatability_note: Field experiment requiring participants to physically visit a corporate gym, tracked via ID badge swipes.

title: Cash transfer narratives and charitable giving
simulatability_note: Survey experiment presenting text narratives and asking participants to allocate a monetary bonus.

title: CHOOSING YOUR POND: LOCATION CHOICES AND RELATIVE INCOME
simulatability_note: Analyzes the real-world ranking and physical relocation choices of medical students applying for residency programs.

title: HOW DOES THE GENDER OF THE VICTIM AFFECT PEOPLE’S MORAL JUDGMENT ABOUT THE DISCRIMINATOR?
simulatability_note: Survey experiment presenting textual scenarios and eliciting participants' moral judgments and attitudes.

title: Forecasting the Results of Experiments
simulatability_note: Survey task wherein participants read descriptions of other studies and provide text-based forecasts for treatment effects.

title: Student loan repayment plan choice
simulatability_note: Survey experiment asking participants to choose between hypothetically framed student loan repayment plans.

title: The Effect of Stay-at-home Orders on Mobility, Economic, and Health Outcomes during the Coronavirus Pandemic
simulatability_note: Survey measuring individuals' beliefs about COVID-19 and assessing self-reported social distancing intentions.

title: Learning about COVID-19: Improving Knowledge via Incentives and Feedback
simulatability_note: Experiment relies on live telephone survey interactions spread across multiple weeks to a population in Mozambique.

title: Dishonesty and self-awareness
simulatability_note: Online experiment that evaluates cheating via a text-based sender-receiver game and matrix task reports.

title: Statistical Fairness Discrimination
simulatability_note: Online survey exposing participants to text vignettes regarding discrimination and evaluating subsequent fairness judgments.

title: Perceived intertemporal inconsistency and demand for commitment in a novel context, volunteering
simulatability_note: Study outcome depends on subjects taking real-world action to volunteer with a non-profit organization over a subsequent month.

title: The Ability to Distill the Truth
simulatability_note: Survey experiment evaluating subjects' ability to accurately discern true/false news headlines presented in text.

title: Machine learning heterogeneous effects in experimental settings
simulatability_note: Participants engage in a real-effort visual task counting zeroes in an image matrix under strict time pressure, which does not cleanly translate to an LLM context.

title: Public administration job attraction
simulatability_note: Online conjoint experiment evaluating preferences across different hypothetical textual job profile attributes.

title: Systematizing inquiry about risk and reward
simulatability_note: Field experiment with real investors evaluating real pitches and making financial assessments regarding startups.

title: Willingness to listen to polarizing views
simulatability_note: Experiment relies entirely on listening to audio recordings of other individuals' voices.

title: Vaccination mandates
simulatability_note: Study relies on observing real-world public health behaviors and physical COVID-19 vaccination timing.

title: Preferences for Giving Versus Preferences for Redistribution
simulatability_note: Survey experiment using dictator games and hypothetical voting prompts easily administered via text.

title: COVID-19 Information Treatments Shift Related Policy Preferences and Plans for Consumer Behavior
simulatability_note: Online survey evaluating text-based policy preferences and behavioral intentions after information treatments.

title: Word definitions
simulatability_note: Online survey testing comprehension and ideologically motivated definitions of text passages.

title: Attention Allocation, Macroeconomic Expectations, and Consumption Behavior
simulatability_note: Online survey presenting inflation forecasts and measuring updates to economic expectations.

title: Trust in AI-generated content
simulatability_note: Survey experiment requiring participants to read text paragraphs and judge their authorship and factual accuracy.
